chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.S- 1562 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For Katcha Peshi. 04.11.2016. Mr. Kamran Haider, Advocate for petitioners alongwith petitioners. Mr. Sher Muhammad Laghari, State Counsel alongwith SIP Muhammad Urs P.S Bhirya City and ASI Sabir Hassan Gopang, P.S A-Section Nawabshah. Mr. Asif Rizwan Kamboh, Advocate for respondents No.7 to 10. -.-.-. The main grievance of the petitioners is that private respondents are causing harassment to the petitioners by not allowing them to use and enjoy their ancestral property. Petitioners have also placed on record the ownership extract of the said property. However, today learned State Counsel has filed parawise comments of police officials wherein, inter alia, while disputing the allegation of causing harassment the official respondents have also undertaken to remain impartial in the matter and to provide protection to any party in accordance with law if they are approached with such a request. Mr. Asif Rizwan Kamboh, Advocate, files vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.7 to 10 and has submitted that a civil dispute is pending between petitioners and private respondents in the shape of Civil Suit No. 168/2015 before IInd Senior Civil Judge, Naushehro Feroz. However, petitioners should have mentioned this fact in the instant petition in order to avoid any adverse consequence on the ground of concealment of facts. Be that as it may, present petition is disposed of with the directions that official respondents will not patronize any party or cause harassment to any of the petitioners and respondents and will discharge their duties in accordance with law.

However, petitioners should have mentioned this fact in the instant petition in order to avoid any adverse consequence on the ground of concealment of facts. Be that as it may, present petition is disposed of with the directions that official respondents will not patronize any party or cause harassment to any of the petitioners and respondents and will discharge their duties in accordance with law. Private respondents are directed not to take law in their hands, however, they are at liberty to pursue their legal remedy in accordance with law. JUDGE Ali Haider
